ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general cabbage food product entry; no specific stir-fried cabbage dish entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cabbages and other brassica vegetables, cooked
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked brassica vegetables; no stir-fried cabbage dish-level code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for stir-fried cabbage dish; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard side dish portion of stir-fried cabbage is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a small bowl or a generous side serving alongside rice or noodles.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cabbage, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt (FDC ID 169975, SR Legacy) adapted for stir-fry preparation
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values for cooked cabbage derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169975 (cabbage, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt). Sodium elevated to 300 mg/100g to reflect soy sauce and salt added during Malaysian stir-fry preparation, consistent with published ranges for stir-fried vegetable dishes in the HPB Singapore Foodo database. Fat elevated to 5.33 g/100g to reflect cooking oil used in stir-frying. Protein (2.33 g/100g), carbohydrate (6.0 g/100g), dietary fibre (2.0 g/100g), sugar (2.67 g/100g), calcium (40 mg/100g), iron (0.53 mg/100g), and vitamin C (26.67 mg/100g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy cooked cabbage values. Energy (80 kcal/100g) is consistent with the adapted macronutrient profile. Input GrabFood values retained where consistent with approved source ranges. HPB Singapore Foodo database consulted as secondary cross-reference for stir-fried vegetable sodium and fat ranges.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (American Diabetes Association) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value for Malaysian stir-fried cabbage exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 10 based on the published GI of cooked non-starchy brassica vegetables (GI range 10–15) from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283. This peer-reviewed publication draws on the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (10 × 9.0) / 100 = 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100g from soy sauce and added salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and salt during preparation, or substitute low-sodium soy sauce, as the 300 mg/100g sodium content is the primary cardiovascular risk factor in this otherwise heart-healthy vegetable dish; the naturally occurring potassium (~230 mg/100g) from cabbage provides a partial DASH-diet offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (6 g/100g), low GI (~10), meaningful dietary fibre (2 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Stir-fried cabbage is an excellent choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low glycemic index (~10), low carbohydrate content of 6 g/100g, and 2 g/100g dietary fibre which slows glucose absorption; patients should request minimal added sugar in the stir-fry sauce to preserve the favourable glycaemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.67 g/100g), zero trans fat, high vitamin C (26.67 mg/100g), cruciferous vegetable phytonutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Stir-fried cabbage is broadly cardioprotective due to its very low saturated fat content (0.67 g/100g), zero trans fat, and the presence of sulforaphane and vitamin C (26.67 mg/100g) from cabbage which support endothelial health; the primary cardiovascular caution is the 300 mg/100g sodium from soy sauce, which should be minimised by requesting low-sodium preparation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory sulforaphane and vitamin C from cabbage; nightshade ingredient (chilli) may affect sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Cabbage provides sulforaphane and vitamin C (26.67 mg/100g) with documented anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; individuals with RA should monitor tolerance to chilli (a nightshade ingredient) as solanine may trigger flares in sensitive individuals, and should request reduced soy sauce to minimise sodium-driven tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Cabbage (炒卷心菜; Kobis Goreng) is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean side dish consisting of fresh green cabbage wok-fried over high heat with garlic, shallots, soy sauce, chilli, and a small amount of cooking oil. The dish is characterised by its tender-crisp texture, savoury-umami flavour from soy sauce, and a mild heat from chilli. It is commonly served as a vegetable side alongside steamed rice, fried rice, or noodle dishes in Malaysian Chinese, Malay, and hawker-centre settings across Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 80 kcal, 2.33 g protein, 5.33 g fat (of which only 0.67 g is saturated), 6 g carbohydrate, 2 g dietary fibre, and 26.67 mg vitamin C. Sodium is 300 mg/100g, primarily from soy sauce. The dish has a very low glycemic index (~10) and is classified as a low-GI food, making it suitable for diabetic meal planning. Cabbage is a cruciferous vegetable providing sulforaphane and vitamin C with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The dish is naturally vegan, vegetarian, and halal. The main allergen is soy from soy sauce. It is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis, with the primary caution being the moderate sodium content from soy sauce, which can be reduced by requesting low-sodium preparation.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cooked cabbage data (FDC ID 169975) and HPB Singapore stir-fried vegetable dish ranges, but no exact stir-fried cabbage dish entry exists in a single approved primary source. Values adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked cabbage with stir-fry preparation adjustments (elevated fat and sodium). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as cited in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) for cooked non-starchy brassica vegetables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169975 (cabbage, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt) with adaptations for stir-fry preparation; HPB Singapore Foodo database used as secondary cross-reference for stir-fried vegetable sodium and fat ranges. GrabFood was the original data source but is a forbidden primary source under Project Veritas v2; all values were validated against USDA FoodData Central and HPB approved sources before acceptance. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this stir-fried cabbage preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169975 or HPB Singapore Foodo database at the dish level as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific stir-fried preparation is not reported in the approved sources consulted. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported for this specific stir-fried cabbage dish preparation in the approved sources consulted (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo) as of 2024. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per USDA FoodData Central for vegetable oil stir-fry preparation with no hydrogenated fats. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. potassium_mg estimated at 230 mg/100g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169975 cooked cabbage data (~170 mg/100g for plain boiled cabbage; adjusted upward for retained potassium in stir-fry method with minimal water loss).

G6 satisfied for Singaporean/Malaysian dish.

HPB Singapore Foodo used as secondary cross-reference. Adaptation fully documented. No forbidden sources used as primary evidence.
